text: This may be exactly what I'm looking for today.  I want a good way for CC to use dashboards for things, mostly internal things that I have hooks for, just to see that all the inside parts are operating properly

text:
There is a version on the web, see if you can get your claude to connect to it, if that works, ask it to export it so you have a local file version, and then in theory it will be able to pair within that local copy, but when its working locally it can also use its normal filesystem tools.

<https://tomlarkworthy.github.io/lopebooks/notebooks/@tomlarkworthy_computational-blogs-with-claude-code-connectivity.html#view=R100%28S70%28%40tomlarkworthy%2Fcomputational-blogs-with-claude-code-connectivity%29%2CC30%28S50%28%40tomlarkworthy%2Fclaude-code-pairing%29%2CS50%28%40tomlarkworthy%2Fmodule-selection%29%29%29|https://tomlarkworthy.github.io/lopebooks/notebooks/@tomlarkworthy_computational-blogs-with-claude-code-connectivity.html#view=R100%2[…]ion%29%29%29>

elements.text: There is a version on the web, see if you can get your claude to connect to it, if that works, ask it to export it so you have a local file version, and then in theory it will be able to pair within that local copy, but when its working locally it can also use its normal filesystem tools.

text: <https://github.com/tomlarkworthy/lopecode-dev> has more substantial prompts but also more difficult setup because I use `safehouse` for sandboxing claude.

text:
Concerning your question <@UC2A2ARPT> at the end of the meetup, my 2 cents:

• If we have about an hour and three slots, it'd make sense to have around 20m per talk + questions. We might go slightly over an hour like this, allowing time for an intro.
• Since 7m of presentation felt a bit too short, what about something like 12m of presenting and 7m of questions?

elements.text: at the end of the meetup, my 2 cents:
If we have about an hour and three slots, it'd make sense to have around 20m per talk + questions. We might go slightly over an hour like this, allowing time for an intro.
Since 7m of presentation felt a bit too short, what about something like 12m of presenting and 7m of questions?

text: <@UC2A2ARPT> I want to share that I appreciate that the system is flexible and you are able to use your discretion to moderate appropriately.  Using the most recent virtual meetup as an example, it was very easy to stay engaged with the presenters for the full 14 minutes and I trust that your spidey-sense would have known if that was not the case and you would have encouraged an appropriate level of audience participation.  Having the freedom to let the meetup unfold in a way that feels genuine is the top priority from my perspective.
